text: Managing a team effectively requires a combination of skills. First, clear communication is essential; team members need to understand their roles and goals. Delegation is equally important—you must assign tasks based on individual strengths to avoid overloading anyone. Providing regular feedback helps people improve and feel valued. Motivation can be boosted by recognizing achievements and creating a positive work environment. When conflicts arise, address them promptly through open discussion and active listening. By focusing on these areas, you can build a cohesive and productive team.
